title: CoverageRunResult — outcome persisted to core.coverage_runs.result
description: JSON Schema for the result JSONB column on core.coverage_runs (src/will/governance/coverage_runner.py). Three distinct run kinds write to this column, producing different shapes; all share a loose-superset schema with additionalProperties:true. Kind is NOT stored on the result dict itself — use core.coverage_runs.target_file (NULL = report run) and core.coverage_runs.batch_priority (non-NULL = batch run) as discriminators. Authority: ADR-056 Wave 3 (persistence-boundary contract). Uses .schema.json suffix — intentionally skipped by intent_validator.py.

ok.description: report kind: True if pytest exited 0 and the report was generated successfully. Absent on generate/batch kinds (they use 'success' instead).

format.description: report kind: The output format requested. Forwarded from the route parameter.

show_missing.description: report kind: Whether line-level missing-coverage details were included.

summary.description: report kind: Captured pytest summary line. Also used as the error message if ok=false.

success.description: generate/batch kinds: True if at least one test was generated without error. Absent on report kind (uses 'ok' instead).

status.description: batch kind: Terminal state from BatchRemediationService. 'no_candidates' = no files below threshold; 'no_matches' = no files passed complexity filter; 'completed' = at least one file processed.

processed.description: batch kind: Number of files the batch loop actually attempted.

results.description: batch kind: Per-file results from BatchRemediationService.process_batch(). Each item has 'file', 'original_coverage', plus per-file action output.

file.description: Repo-relative path of the source file processed.

original_coverage.description: Coverage percentage at time of selection.

error.description: generate kind: Error message if the single-file remediation failed. Absent on success.
